metaTitle: Radiators Not Heating Up | Common Causes and Fixes
metaDescription: Radiators not heating up? Learn the common causes, safe checks and when to book a heating engineer for repair.
h1: Why Are My Radiators Not Heating Up?
intro: If your boiler seems to be running but the radiators are staying cold, the issue may be with system pressure, circulation, controls or individual radiator valves rather than the boiler alone.
safetyWarning: If multiple radiators stay cold or the heating is uneven throughout the property, the system needs checking before it worsens.
symptoms: Radiators are cold while the boiler is on
Some radiators are hot and others stay cold
Heating is slow and uneven around the property
safeChecks: Check that the heating is actually set to come on at the controls
Check the boiler pressure if your system uses a sealed circuit
Check whether individual radiator valves are turned on
doNotDiy: Do not open the boiler to inspect pumps or internal parts
Do not force stuck valves with tools if they will not move cleanly
Do not assume the boiler is the only possible cause
typicalFixes: Replacing a faulty thermostatic radiator valve

sections.heading: What cold radiators can indicate
sections.body: Radiators that are not heating up can point to poor circulation, a control issue, pressure loss or local valve problems, depending on whether one radiator is affected or the whole property.

sections.heading: Why some systems show mixed symptoms
sections.body: You may see one warm radiator and several cold ones, or heat upstairs but not downstairs. That pattern helps narrow down whether the problem is local to a valve or broader within the system.

sections.heading: When to book heating diagnosis
sections.body: If the controls are set correctly and the radiators are still cold, it is worth booking diagnosis so the system problem can be identified rather than guessed at.
